leadOwned.reason: Mutable workflow state, packet publication and operation artifacts belong to the controller.
Controller-owned refreeze support for preserving compatible accepted tasks across plan adoption.
Controller-owned CLI flag for compatible accepted-task preservation during refreeze adoption.
Controller-owned regression coverage for compatible accepted-task preservation during refreeze adoption.
Controller-owned public workflow export for finalization.
Controller-owned next-action projection for FINAL_AUDIT finalization.

requirements.description: The offline release-candidate shall assemble a content-addressed local release payload from the current source tree, shared skills, adapters, conformance corpus, documentation and governance files; verify two deterministic inventory passes over the same payload bytes; publish no production-ready or verified-external claim; and leave production promotion blocked until signed external release authorities exist.

Offline release-candidate readiness shall run local current-tree and release-payload neutrality scans with zero findings under the portable policy. Signed all-refs, reflog, unreachable-object and all-archive release neutrality remains a deferred production-promotion gate and shall not be claimed by the offline candidate.

The kit shall publish CI workflow and support-matrix contracts for Ubuntu, macOS and Windows on CPython 3.11, 3.12 and 3.13 as production-promotion requirements. The offline candidate validates the matrix contract and adapter EXPERIMENTAL maturity locally without claiming authenticated matrix leg receipts.

The kit shall publish a provider-neutral live cost calibration contract as a deferred production-promotion gate. The offline candidate validates the deterministic synthetic baseline and documents the required external environment and receipt inputs without claiming live model execution.

task.launchGate: LG-14 WS-08 through WS-13 and WS-16 accepted, r13 finalization-capable source tree present, and the local controller pre-launch gate passed; no external release authority is required for the offline candidate
